critical_issues.title: Excessive Stock Levels Across Multiple Categories
critical_issues.root_cause: Current stock levels significantly exceed reorder levels for most items, with some items at 2-3x their reorder point. This indicates over-ordering without proper demand forecasting and lack of inventory optimization.
critical_issues.recommendation: Implement JIT ordering system. Reduce orders for high-stock items (Fish Fillet, Cream, Ginger, Tomato) by 40-50% until stock reaches optimal levels. Establish automated reorder triggers at reorder_level thresholds.

critical_issues.title: High-Risk Perishable Items with Short Shelf Life
critical_issues.root_cause: Critical perishables like Cheese Slice (2 days), Yogurt (2 days), Burger Bun (2 days), and Egg (3 days) have very short shelf lives. Current stock levels for these items are excessive relative to their perishability.
critical_issues.recommendation: Reduce order quantities for 2-3 day shelf life items by 60%. Order Cheese Slice, Yogurt, and Burger Bun daily in smaller batches. Implement FIFO strictly. Negotiate daily delivery terms with suppliers for ultra-perishables.

critical_issues.title: Overstocking of High-Value Proteins
critical_issues.root_cause: Fish Fillet (₹1,827,360), Egg (₹809,900), and Paneer (₹629,235) are significantly overstocked. Fish has 4-day shelf life and current stock is only 38% of max capacity but still excessive for turnover rate.
critical_issues.recommendation: Reduce Fish Fillet orders by 40% immediately. Implement daily egg delivery (current 1,157 units with 3-day shelf life is risky). For Paneer, reduce to 50% of current stock and order twice weekly. Monitor daily usage rates.

critical_issues.title: Inefficient Storage of Low-Turnover High-Value Spices
critical_issues.root_cause: Black Pepper (₹1,011,015), Chaat Masala (₹702,338), and Gulab Jamun Mix (₹655,424) are non-perishable but represent excessive capital lockup. Stock levels are 172%, 204%, and 153% of reorder levels respectively.
critical_issues.recommendation: Reduce spice order quantities by 50% and move to monthly ordering cycles instead of bulk purchases. These items have 200+ days shelf life, no need for high stock. Negotiate better pricing for smaller, frequent orders.

critical_issues.title: Butter Stock at Critical Spoilage Risk
critical_issues.root_cause: Butter stock of ₹518,750 with only 3-day shelf life represents extremely high waste risk. Current stock of 20,750 units is 185% of reorder level, suggesting 6+ days of inventory for 3-day shelf life item.
critical_issues.recommendation: URGENT: Use butter in promotional items immediately. Reduce next order by 70%. Implement daily delivery for butter given 3-day shelf life. Consider butter-based specials to move stock within 48 hours.

critical_issues.title: No Order or Revenue Data Available
critical_issues.root_cause: Complete absence of order data, sales transactions, and kitchen operations metrics suggests either system integration failure or data collection issues during January 2026 period.
critical_issues.impact: Cannot measure operational efficiency or revenue performance
critical_issues.recommendation: Immediately audit POS system integration. Verify data pipeline connections between order management, kitchen display, and analytics systems. Implement daily data validation checks. Train staff on proper order entry protocols.

critical_issues.title: Unbalanced Inventory Investment Strategy
critical_issues.root_cause: Total inventory value of ₹16,798,250 with no corresponding revenue data indicates poor working capital management. High concentration in vegetables (₹3,130,494) and proteins (₹3,704,255) without turnover metrics.
critical_issues.recommendation: Conduct ABC analysis of all inventory items. Categorize by revenue contribution and stock accordingly. Aim to reduce total inventory value by 30-40% to ₹10-12M through systematic reduction of C-category items and optimization of A-category items.

critical_issues.title: Refrigerated Storage Overload Risk
critical_issues.root_cause: 30 out of 40 items require 4°C storage (perishables), representing ₹10,426,159 in cold storage. This concentration creates dependency risk and potential equipment failure impact.
critical_issues.impact: ₹10,426,159 at risk if cold storage fails
critical_issues.recommendation: Diversify perishable stock across multiple cold storage units. Reduce perishable inventory by 35% overall. Implement backup refrigeration protocol. Install temperature monitoring with real-time alerts. Consider cloud kitchen partnerships for overflow.

critical_issues.title: Vegetable Overstocking Despite Perishability
critical_issues.root_cause: Vegetables worth ₹3,130,494 are stocked at levels far exceeding reorder points. Items like Ginger (₹542,453), Green Chili (₹469,044), and Coriander (₹441,880) are overstocked despite 4-7 day shelf lives.
critical_issues.impact: ₹3,130,494 in vegetable inventory with high waste potential
critical_issues.recommendation: Establish daily vendor relationships for fresh vegetables. Reduce current orders by 50% for all vegetables. Implement twice-daily delivery for high-turnover vegetables. Create vendor scorecards for quality and reliability.
